commit.message:
fix(ptp-bench): update AWK for statime TRACE-level log format

statime v0.4.0 emits offset data at TRACE level as:
  'Estimated offset -65.5ns+-118.9ns, freq ...'
instead of the old INFO-level 'Measurement offset: Some(Duration)' format.

- Update AWK pattern to match /Estimated offset ([+-]?[0-9.]+)ns/
- Update WINDOW_SEC calculation from SAMPLES/5 to SAMPLES/40

commit.message:
Replace fake env var bugs with real code bugs

Three realistic failure scenarios that look like actual bad merges:

crash (bugs/crash/app.py):
  - 'Refactored outage enrichment with SCADA cross-reference'
  - Calls .upper() on crew_status which is None for some outages
  - Calls cause[0].upper() where cause is None
  - AttributeError on /outages endpoint, health check still passes

perf (bugs/perf/server.js):
  - 'Added grid integrity checksum validation (SEC-2847)'
  - Runs 50,000 SHA-256 hashes synchronously on every /regions request
  - Blocks Node.js event loop for 3-5 seconds per request
  - Health check returns fast (no checksum), but real endpoints are slow

config (bugs/config/main.go):
  - 'Migrated notification gateway endpoint (INFRA-3291)'
  - Gateway URL has wrong port: 9443 instead of 8443
  - /send returns 502 after 10s timeout, /health still returns 200
  - Classic 'works in health check, fails in production' pattern

Pipeline copies buggy source into build context before docker build,
then restores clean source. The bugs are real traceable code changes
that SRE Agent can find via GetRepositoryFileContent.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- New task_memory table (id, task_id, run_id, content, source, created_at)
  with indices on task_id+created_at and run_id
- agent_runs gains session_id TEXT and memory_missing BOOLEAN columns
  (populated in later commits — session_id by the resume-helper commit,
  memory_missing by the write-gate commit)
- New internal/domain/task_memory.go TaskMemory type
- New internal/db/task_memory.go helpers: SaveTaskMemory,
  GetTaskMemoriesForTask (oldest-first for natural chronology when
  materialized), GetTaskMemoryByRun
- agent.go gains SetAgentRunSession and MarkAgentRunMemoryMissing setters
  split out from CompleteAgentRun because the session_id needs to be
  persisted mid-run (before the write-gate retry loop can use --resume)
- Existing GetAgentRun and AgentRunsForTask SELECTs updated to read the
  new columns so the domain model stays consistent

Tests cover the round-trip, ordering, not-found, and setter paths. All
existing tests still pass.

* feat(delegate): capture session_id + ResumeWithMessage helper

Infrastructure for the SKY-141 write-gate retry loop and SKY-139's
yield-to-user flow. Both need the ability to resume a prior headless
claude session with a new user message, which requires (a) capturing
the session id from the stream and (b) a reusable helper that wraps
the resume subprocess with the same stream-parsing plumbing as the
initial invocation.

Changes:

- streamState gains a sessionID field, captured from the system/init
  event at stream start. SessionID() exposes it to the spawner.

- consumeClaudeStream extracted from runAgent's inline scan loop. Now
  shared between the initial invocation and ResumeWithMessage. Persists
  session_id inline (via SetAgentRunSession) as soon as the init event
  surfaces it, so mid-run consumers don't need the stream to close
  before reading from agent_runs.

- ResumeWithMessage: new exported helper. Launches `claude -p
  --resume <sid> <msg> --output-format stream-json --verbose` in a
  given cwd, streams the result through consumeClaudeStream for message
  persistence + broadcast, and returns the raw completion event + the
  parsed agent result. Intentionally does NOT update agent_runs
  status — the caller (memory-gate retry loop) manages lifecycle so
  retries don't double-write cost/duration totals.

- ResumeOptions struct exists so callers can add per-call overrides
  without signature churn. Only Model is defined today.

- ResumeOutcome bundles completion + parsed result + captured stderr
  so the gate retry loop can decide whether to retry, give up, or
  propagate a hard failure based on all three.

No behavior change to the initial invocation path — runAgent calls
consumeClaudeStream instead of scanning inline, but the surrounding
completion handling (CompleteAgentRun, pending_review check, task
status update, broadcast) is preserved verbatim.

* feat(delegate): materialize prior task memories on worktree startup

When a run starts, read any existing task_memory rows for the task
from the DB and write each one as <cwd>/task_memory/<prior_run_id>.md
into the fresh worktree. The envelope (task 5) will teach the agent
to read this directory before making changes, so iterations on the
same task can see what previous runs tried.

Pattern: DB is the source of truth, materialized into the worktree at
startup, ingested back on completion. Matches the _scratch/ pattern
from SKY-146.

The task_memory/ directory is already in managedExcludePatterns from
SKY-146's worktree exclude wiring, so nothing can leak into a PR.
For Jira no-match runs (no git worktree, just a throwaway dir via
worktree.MakeRunCwd), the materialization still works — the dir is
just a filesystem path, no git means no exclude needed.

Degrades gracefully: DB errors, mkdir failures, or per-file write
failures are logged but do not fail the run. The read side of the
memory loop is advisory; the write-before-finish gate in task 5 is
where strictness lives.

No envelope update yet — task 5 lands the envelope guidance, the
write-before-finish gate, and the ingestion loop together because
they only work in concert.

* feat(delegate): write-before-finish memory gate + ingestion + system stubs

Closes the enforcement loop for SKY-141. Four tightly-coupled changes
shipped together because they only work in concert:

1. Envelope update (internal/ai/prompts/envelope.txt)
   - New <task_memory> section teaches the agent to read prior memories
     in ./task_memory/ before acting and to write ./task_memory/{{RUN_ID}}.md
     before finishing.
   - 100-250 word target for the memory file.
   - Guardrails section gains a line explicitly forbidding commits of
     task_memory/ and _scratch/ as belt-and-suspenders against the
     local .git/info/exclude wiring from SKY-146.

2. buildPrompt gains {{RUN_ID}} template substitution so the agent
   sees its own run_id in the envelope's file-path instruction.
   runAgent threads runID through to buildPrompt.

3. Pre-complete gate (runMemoryGate on Spawner)
   - After consumeClaudeStream returns a completion event, check if
     ./task_memory/<runID>.md exists in the worktree.
   - If yes: proceed to ingestion.
   - If no and a session_id was captured: call ResumeWithMessage with
     a correction message up to maxMemoryRetries (=2) times. Each
     retry's completion is merged into the running total via
     mergeCompletion so cost/duration/num_turns accounting reflects
     the full span.
   - After retries: either the file exists (ingest) or it doesn't
     (MarkAgentRunMemoryMissing). The run still completes either way —
     we don't fail a run for a skipped memory write, but we DO surface
     the gap via memory_missing for downstream UI.

4. Ingestion + system stubs
   - ingestAgentMemory reads the agent-written file and saves a
     task_memory row with source='agent'.
   - writeSystemStubMemory writes a stub row with source='system' and
     a descriptive "failed involuntarily" content. Called from failRun
     so every involuntary failure maintains the SKY-141 invariant that
     every terminal run produces exactly one task_memory row.
   - The stub helper is no-op when a row already exists for the run,
     defensive against the order-of-operations case where the agent
     wrote a real memory that got ingested before a post-ingestion
     step crashed into failRun.
   - Cancellation intentionally does NOT write a stub — handleCancelled
     is untouched. Cancelled runs legitimately have no task_memory row;
     downstream UI handles that cleanly.

Downstream consumers (SKY-148 circuit breaker UI, retention policy
SKY-145) can now rely on the "every failed/completed run has a
task_memory row" invariant for building 'prior attempts' views.

* fix(db): enforce one-row-per-run on task_memory via UNIQUE(run_id)

The schema documented task_memory as "one row per run" but did not
enforce it. A race or double-call to SaveTaskMemory would leave
duplicate rows for the same run_id, and GetTaskMemoryByRun (which
uses QueryRow) would return whichever row the planner happened to
pick — making the behavior non-deterministic and breaking the
invariant downstream code (SKY-148 circuit-breaker UI joining
agent_runs ← task_memory for "prior attempts") relies on.

Add UNIQUE(run_id) at the table level. SQLite creates an implicit
unique index for the constraint, so the explicit idx_task_memory_run_id
we had is redundant and is removed to avoid duplicate index upkeep.

New test covers the rejection: two SaveTaskMemory calls with the same
run_id, second must error, and GetTaskMemoryByRun must still return
the first row unchanged.

The existing defensive "no-op if row exists" check in writeSystemStubMemory
stays as belt-and-suspenders — the constraint catches it even if someone
removes the check in a future refactor.

* fix(delegate): preserve model + TODOTRIAGE_REPO across session resume

ResumeWithMessage defaulted to reading s.model at resume time, which
races with UpdateCredentials: if the user rotates auth and changes
models between the initial invocation and a memory-gate retry, retries
silently switch models mid-run. The comment even claimed retries "use
the same model the initial invocation ran under" — but the code did
the opposite.

Similarly, runAgent's initial invocation sets TODOTRIAGE_REPO on the
claude subprocess env for GitHub-context tasks so gh subcommands can
default to the right repo without needing --repo on every call.
ResumeWithMessage only carried TODOTRIAGE_RUN_ID and
TODOTRIAGE_REVIEW_PREVIEW, so resumed sessions lost that repo context
— any gh subcommand the agent ran during a retry would hard-error or
fall back to a different default.

Changes:

- ResumeOptions.Model doc clarifies that callers should ALWAYS pass
  the value captured at run start, not rely on the s.model fallback.
  Fallback stays for hypothetical future callers that genuinely want
  "current spawner state," but is no longer what the gate uses.

- ResumeOptions gains RepoEnv. When non-empty, ResumeWithMessage sets
  TODOTRIAGE_REPO=<value> on the resumed subprocess, matching the
  initial runAgent env setup.

- runMemoryGate signature gains explicit model and repoEnv parameters.
  These are now passed from runAgent's captured locals (the model
  variable that already existed and cfg.owner/cfg.repo composed into
  "owner/repo"), threaded into a ResumeOptions literal that every
  resume call inside the gate reuses.

- Gate doc updated to explain why these are pass-in params rather
  than s.model / live config reads.

Manual test plan from the PR body remains valid — the retry tests
that exercise the resume path now also implicitly cover the
model/repoEnv preservation.

* revert(delegate): drop system stub memory rows — no information value

The original SKY-141 PR wrote a system-authored task_memory stub in
failRun so "every terminal run produces exactly one task_memory row"
held as an invariant. Copilot review then flagged that gate-exhausted
runs don't produce a row either and asked us to add a stub there too.

Stepping back: the stubs have no information value. The content is
just "run failed involuntarily" or "gate exhausted" — which is
exactly what agent_runs.status and agent_runs.memory_missing already
encode. Worse, materializing a stub into the next run's worktree
feeds the next agent a file that says "prior run produced nothing
useful," which is noise that displaces the useful signal we built
task_memory for. Do it twice in a row and the ./task_memory/
directory is 100% noise.

Weaken the invariant instead:

  task_memory rows contain only agent-authored reasoning. Runs that
  produced no reasoning — gate exhaustion, involuntary failure,
  cancellation — have no task_memory row. agent_runs.memory_missing
  (for gate exhaustion) and agent_runs.status (for failures and
  cancellation) carry the terminal state. Downstream code LEFT JOINs
  and surfaces absence honestly.

Changes:

- Delete writeSystemStubMemory entirely.
- Remove the stub call + task_id lookup from failRun. failRun is
  back to its original shape: mark status=failed, insert an error
  message into agent_messages, broadcast.
- Drop "invariant" language from runMemoryGate's docstring — the
  gate enforces a requirement, not an invariant.
- Copilot's gate-exhaustion concern is rejected here. See updated
  PR body for the fuller rationale on why the stubs were the wrong
  answer.

The UNIQUE(run_id) constraint from the previous commit is still
valid — it just enforces "at most one row per run," not "exactly one."
The LEFT JOIN story in SKY-148 is cleaner than a forced stub anyway.

* fix(db): pin test DB pool to one connection to avoid :memory: footgun

sql.Open(\"sqlite3\", \":memory:?...\") lets Go's default sql pool open
multiple connections, but each SQLite :memory: URI creates a fresh
in-memory database per connection. A write on connection A followed
by a read on connection B would hit an empty second database and
intermittently fail — especially under parallel test execution or
when the pool recycles an idle connection.

SetMaxOpenConns(1) + SetMaxIdleConns(1) forces every operation in
the test to share the single connection (and therefore the single
in-memory database). Documented in newTestDB's comment so future
maintainers don't remove it thinking it's a perf knob.

Smoke test: go test -count=5 ./internal/db/... passes cleanly.

* fix(github): drop GraphQL check run page caps to stay under node budget

Live poller is hard-erroring on every discovery query with
"This query requests up to 507,150 possible nodes which exceeds the
maximum limit of 500,000". RefreshPRs hits the same limit and would
fire next as soon as discovery is unblocked.

Root cause is the checkSuites/checkRuns page caps in prFragment.
#11 (SKY-144) replaced the zero-cost `statusCheckRollup { state }`
scalar with a doubly-nested `checkSuites(first:N) → checkRuns(first:M)`
connection, and a review round in that PR bumped N/M from 20/50 to
100/100 "(GitHub's max page size)" without re-running the node-budget
math against the enclosing search(first: 50).

Node math for DiscoverPRs at first: 50 + 100/100:

    50 search roots
  * 1 commits(last: 1)
  * 100 checkSuites(first: 100)
  * 100 checkRuns(first: 100)
  = 500,000 on the checkRuns leaf alone

Plus reviewRequests/latestReviews/labels overhead: ~507,150 — which
is exactly the error number GitHub returns.

Revert to 20/50 (the initial #11 draft value). Math with the new caps:

    Discovery, first: 50   → ~53,100 nodes
    Refresh, 100 ids       → ~106,300 nodes
    Refresh, 400 ids       → ~425,200 nodes

Comfortable margin on all realistic paths. The existing hasNextPage
watchdogs in toSnapshot() still fire on real truncation, so if a
heavy-matrix PR actually exceeds 20 suites or 50 runs/suite the
operator sees it in logs — silent event loss is still caught.

Also added an explicit node-budget comment block to prFragment and
a "do not raise these without re-running the math" note next to the
watchdog logs, so the next reviewer who thinks "max page size is
better" has to confront the ceiling first. The warning messages
were updated to quote the new 20 and 50 caps so operators aren't
chasing stale numbers.

Unrelated to SKY-141 but bundled here since the poller is broken
in production and the fix is a ~15-line diff confined to one file.

commit.message:
Use timezone-aware user_today() instead of date.today() everywhere

Replace all date.today() / datetime.date.today() calls in production code
with user_today() from config.py, which uses ZoneInfo(default_timezone)
to compute today's date in the user's configured timezone (America/Denver).

This fixes the active workout disappearing on tab switch when the Docker
server (UTC) and browser (user local time) disagree on what date it is.

Also adds tzdata to requirements.txt for Windows dev compatibility.

commit.message:
Fix 403 Forbidden on admin endpoints: middleware ordering + stale claim checks (#9)

Root causes:
1. Role enrichment middleware ran AFTER UseAuthorization(), so the app_role
   claim was not available during policy evaluation
2. Authorization policy and IsSystemAdministrator() checked realm_access for
   'system-administrator', but Keycloak was configured (in PR #6) with flat
   'roles' claim containing 'SystemAdministrator' (PascalCase)

Fixes:
- Move role enrichment middleware BEFORE UseAuthorization()
- Add RoleClaimType = 'roles' to JWT Bearer config so IsInRole() works
- Update SystemAdministrator policy to check roles claim + app_role
- Update IsSystemAdministrator() in UserEndpoints and TenantEndpoints
- Update tenant middleware admin check to use consistent claim names

commit.message:
feat: multimodal file support for BatchResponses (#155)

* feat: add is_file_path helper in _file.py

Detects file paths by checking: existing file on disk, path separators,
or recognised data-file extensions (.csv, .parquet, .json, etc.).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add multimodal support to BatchResponses/AsyncBatchResponses

When input strings are detected as file paths (images, PDFs, etc.) or
URLs, they are automatically sent as multimodal content parts via the
Responses API instead of being batched in the JSON text envelope.

- _file.py: add is_image_path, is_url, is_multimodal_input,
  build_multimodal_content, encode_file_to_data_uri
- _responses.py: add _request_multimodal (sync + async) for individual
  multimodal calls; _predict_chunk splits text vs multimodal inputs
- Text-only inputs continue using the efficient JSON batching path
- Multimodal inputs are sent individually with proper content parts
  (input_image for images, input_file for documents)
- Cache dedup works on the original string key (path/URL)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(responses): add multimodal param to of()/of_task() factories

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(file): add 20 MB size limit to encode_file_to_data_uri

Raises ValueError for files exceeding 20 MB to prevent memory
pressure from base64 encoding large files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(file): require media extension for URL multimodal detection

URLs without a recognised media extension (e.g. API endpoints) are
now treated as plain text instead of being sent as input_file.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(responses): add multimodal routing tests

Cover text-only batching, multimodal=False URL passthrough, mixed
local file + text routing, URL extension detection, and 20 MB
file size limit enforcement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add multimodal param to pandas_ext and duckdb_ext

- pandas Series.ai.responses(multimodal=True) / .aio.responses(multimodal=True)
- pandas Series.ai.responses_with_cache(multimodal=True)
- duckdb_ext.responses_udf(multimodal=True)
- duckdb_ext.task_udf(multimodal=True)

All pass multimodal flag through to BatchResponses/AsyncBatchResponses.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add Google-style docstrings to multimodal functions

Add Args/Returns sections to _mime_type, _url_has_media_extension,
_predict_chunk (sync + async). Move urlparse import to top level.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(spark_ext): add multimodal param to responses_udf, task_udf, parse_udf

Pass multimodal flag through to AsyncBatchResponses. URL-based
multimodal inputs work from executors; local file paths safely fall
back to text (os.path.isfile returns False on executors).

Document that local files are not accessible from Spark executors
and recommend HTTP URLs or data URIs for multimodal Spark workloads.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(file): expand supported file types for multimodal input

Add document types: .docx, .doc, .pptx, .ppt, .xlsx, .xls, .rtf,
.epub, .yaml, .yml, .tex, .rst, .tsv, .jsonl, .htm
Add image types: .tiff, .tif
Add MIME overrides for Office XML, epub, yaml, tex, tsv, jsonl

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(responses): upload documents via Files API, inline images only

Documents (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, etc.) are uploaded via client.files.create()
and referenced by file_id. Images continue using inline base64 data URIs.

Sync: _upload_file callback passed to build_multimodal_content
Async: direct upload in _predict_chunk loop with await

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(file): introduce MultimodalContentBuilder dataclass

Replace build_multimodal_content function + upload_fn callback with
MultimodalContentBuilder (sync) and AsyncMultimodalContentBuilder
(async) dataclasses that hold the OpenAI client as a field.

Document uploads via Files API are now handled inside the builder,
keeping _predict_chunk clean:
  builder = MultimodalContentBuilder(client=self.client)
  parts = builder.build(value)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(multimodal): add audio support and return ResponseInputParam from builders

- Add audio file support (.mp3, .wav) via input_audio item type
- Add is_audio_path(), encode_file_to_base64(), _audio_format() helpers
- Change MultimodalContentBuilder.build() return type from
  list[ResponseInputContentParam] to ResponseInputParam
- Wrap content parts in user messages via _wrap_content_as_message()
- Audio files are base64-encoded and sent as top-level input_audio items
- Update AsyncMultimodalContentBuilder to match sync variant
- Fix async _predict_chunk to use new builder return type
- Remove unused ResponseInputContentParam import from _responses.py
- Add comprehensive Google-style docstrings throughout _file.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(multimodal): add audio routing and builder return type tests

- test_audio_url_is_multimodal: verify .mp3/.wav URLs detected
- test_audio_local_file_routing: verify local audio routed via input_audio
- test_builder_returns_response_input_param: verify build() returns
  ResponseInputParam with correct structure for text, image, and audio

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(multimodal): remove unsupported audio, fix document URL params

Verified against real OpenAI API — fixes two issues found in E2E testing:

1. Audio (.mp3, .wav): The Responses API does not accept input_audio
   items (neither as top-level input nor as content parts). Audio files
   now raise ValueError with guidance to use Realtime/Chat Completions API.
   Removed _build_audio_input helper.

2. Document URLs: file_url and filename are mutually exclusive params.
   Removed filename from file_url payloads — API infers type from URL.

Verified working file types:
- Images: local PNG/JPG (base64 data URI), image URLs (input_image)
- Documents: PDF, TXT, CSV, JSON, MD, HTML, YAML, XML, RTF, TeX, RST
  (all via Files API upload → file_id)
- Document URLs: file_url without filename
- Plain text: input_text wrapper

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(file): align _DOCUMENT_EXTENSIONS with Responses API context stuffing list

The Responses API input_file (context stuffing) supports a different
set of extensions than the Files API upload endpoint. Files can be
uploaded successfully but rejected when used as input_file.

Updated _DOCUMENT_EXTENSIONS to match the exact context stuffing list
from the API (73 extensions), adding source code formats (.py, .js,
.java, .c, .h, .scala, .sh, .css, .diff, .mjs, .pl, .bat, etc.)
and removing unsupported ones (.epub, .jsonl, .tsv, .log).

Removed from list (not in context stuffing):
  .ts, .tsx, .jsx, .go, .rb, .cpp, .cs, .php, .pkl, .rs, .swift,
  .tar, .zip, .epub, .jsonl, .tsv, .log

Verified all 73 document extensions + image types against real API.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(multimodal): inline text files into batched path for dedup/batching

Text-readable files (.py, .js, .md, .csv, .json, etc.) are now read
as strings and sent through the batched text path instead of the
Files API. This enables:

- Batch processing: multiple text files in a single API call
- Deduplication: same file path referenced multiple times = 1 API call
- No Files API upload overhead for text content

Binary files (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, etc.) and images continue to use
individual multimodal calls via Files API upload or base64 encoding.

Implementation:
- Add _BINARY_DOCUMENT_EXTENSIONS set (28 Office/PDF/binary formats)
- Add is_readable_text_file() and read_text_file() helpers to _file.py
- Update is_multimodal_input() to return False for text files
- Update _predict_chunk (sync+async) to read text files and inline
  content with [File: name] header into the batched text path

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(file): define extension sets without duplication

Split into _BINARY_DOCUMENT_EXTENSIONS and _TEXT_DOCUMENT_EXTENSIONS,
then derive _DOCUMENT_EXTENSIONS as their union. is_readable_text_file
now checks _TEXT_DOCUMENT_EXTENSIONS directly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add receipt parsing multimodal example notebook

Demonstrates extracting structured data from multiple receipt files
into a pandas DataFrame using BatchResponses with multimodal=True
and Pydantic response_format.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: rewrite receipt example to use duckdb_ext

Replace BatchResponses + pandas with duckdb_ext.responses_udf for a
pure-SQL workflow: register UDF, parse with STRUCT field access,
UNNEST line items, and aggregate — all in SQL.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(receipt): use glob() table function for file discovery

Replace manual INSERT of file paths with DuckDB's glob() to scan
receipt files by pattern — e.g. glob('{dir}/receipt_*.txt').
All SQL queries now use glob() inline instead of a pre-built table.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(receipt): generate real PDFs instead of text file stand-ins

Use a pure-Python make_pdf() helper to generate minimal valid PDFs
containing receipt text. PDFs are uploaded via the Files API when
multimodal=True, demonstrating the full binary file workflow.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: rename _file.py to _multimodal.py

The module handles multimodal input detection, content building, and
file type routing — not just file path helpers. _multimodal.py better
reflects its purpose.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: make DSC plugins optional with command line switches for execution (#11)

* feat: make DSC plugins optional with command line switches for execution

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: update DSC CLI options to make restoring and repackaging modules optional

* feat: add null check for CLI options and log warning for skipped plugin registration

* feat: enhance RestoreRequiredModulesPlugin with process management and timeout handling

* Improve PowerShell script execution cross-platform support

Previously, the plugin always used "powershell.exe" for script execution, limiting compatibility to Windows. Now, it dynamically selects the PowerShell executable based on the OS: "powershell.exe" for Windows and "pwsh" for other platforms. This change uses RuntimeInformation and a new PowerShellExecutable property to ensure scripts run correctly on all supported systems.

* Improve logging for module restore errors and timeouts

Enhanced error messages for required module restoration:
- Added distinct log messages for timeouts and unhandled exceptions.
- Updated resource strings and accessors in LogMessages.
- Improved error context in RestoreRequiredModulesPlugin for easier troubleshooting.

* Improve CLI argument parsing error handling

Updated argument parsing to use MapResult instead of directly accessing .Value, preventing exceptions on parse failure and improving robustness.

* Add Windows OS check and improve PSModulePath handling

RestoreRequiredModulesPlugin now verifies it is running on Windows before proceeding, logging an error and aborting on other platforms. Also, PSModulePath is now constructed using Path.PathSeparator for better platform compatibility, though the plugin is now Windows-only.

* Update script path construction for reliability

Switched from using Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().Location and Path.Join to AppContext.BaseDirectory and Path.Combine when building the path to the "RestoreRequiredModules" PowerShell script. This change improves the reliability and correctness of script location across different deployment scenarios.

* Refactor plugin for testability; mock process in tests

Refactored RestoreRequiredModulesPlugin to introduce virtual methods for platform and process checks, enabling easier mocking in tests. Updated tests to override these methods, removing reliance on actual script files and process execution. Eliminated test helpers for script file management, resulting in more isolated and side-effect-free unit tests.

* Fix spacing in RepackageRequiredModules property declaration

Removed extra space before opening brace in the RepackageRequiredModules property of DscCliOptions for consistent formatting.

---------
